scenarios.name: silent_collision
scenarios.response_text:
B — events

Both PRs modify the events table: Alan’s PR 312 is “events table migration + webhook status tracking,” and Sarah’s PR 313 is “event lifecycle tracking for API v2,” which adds fields to the events table.

scenarios.name: calendar_blindspot
scenarios.response_text: C — Sarah will be unavailable because she is off Thursday March 13 (she only works M/W/F and has days off on Tue 11 and Thu 13).

scenarios.name: unasked_question
scenarios.response_text:
B

SAML SSO is explicitly out of scope for this phase—“No SAML in this phase,” and it would add ~3 weeks, so it’s not covered by the current release. (2026-02-20 logs)

scenarios.name: misread_metric
scenarios.response_text: B) The new flow removed 4 steps including 'first milestone' (the activation metric trigger), so 'completion' now means something different. This changes what is being measured, making apples-to-apples comparison invalid.

scenarios.name: budget_interpreter
scenarios.response_text: C — staging-3. It was created for load testing but was never actually used for load testing (last deploy Jan 22), so it’s the candidate to decommission to cut costs.

scenarios.name: three_ticket_pattern
scenarios.response_text: B — All three were in migration batch-7, run on 2026-02-25. Evidence: Each ticket (1041, 1043, 1047) lists “Last migration batch: batch-7, run 2026-02-25.”

scenarios.name: meeting_assassin
scenarios.response_text: B — There are 2 open questions: cursor vs offset pagination and webhook payload format; everything else in the design doc has implicit consensus.

scenarios.name: first_day_briefing
scenarios.response_text: B) src/billing/mapping.rs — Alan calls it the "rosetta stone file" and it maps between plan, tier, and subscription naming conventions.

scenarios.name: scope_surgeon
scenarios.response_text:
B

Explanation: Josh’s customer research shows all three prospects (Acme, Pinnacle, Waverly) need CSV; Excel was requested by the sales rep, not the customer, and no one specifically needs PDF. CSV with the right columns is sufficient.

scenarios.name: green_test_trap
scenarios.response_text:
C

0 edge case tests and 7 end-to-end tests. The test distribution shows Edge case tests: 0 (0%) and End-to-end tests: 7 (2%).

scenarios.name: thread_therapist
scenarios.response_text:
B

Sarah proposed a synthesis: JWT tokens that feel like API keys—long-lived JWTs with bearer auth—combining JWT security (rotation, revocation) with API-key simplicity for developers.

scenarios.name: silent_failure_premortem
scenarios.response_text: C — No delivery confirmation mechanism. The plan notes that even if a webhook returns 200, there’s no way to verify it was actually processed, leading to silent failures that aren’t detected until reconciliation reveals mismatches. This is explicitly called out in the webhook section.

scenarios.name: timezone_play
scenarios.response_text:
B

Takeshi at NovaTech (the customer advisory) should weigh in. The current state notes that no one has asked the customer perspective yet, and Takeshi has offered to review from an API consumer perspective.

scenarios.name: debt_ledger
scenarios.response_text:
C

Explanation: 1h (hardcoded URL) + 45m (retry logic) + 1h (generic error messages) = 2.75h/week, plus missed bugs from the skipped test — matching the stated total: ~2.75 hours/week (+ missed bugs).

scenarios.name: competitor_signal
scenarios.response_text: C — 2 prospects (Acme Corp and Pinnacle Inc); Sarah already has a webhook signature module, so adding Ed25519 is localized to that module and would take roughly half a day.

scenarios.name: roi_translator
scenarios.response_text:
C

Explanation: The projection shows break-even at feature 5 (both paths = 25 days). Net positive begins with feature 6 (30 vs 27 days).

scenarios.name: reverse_escalation
scenarios.response_text:
B

Explanation: The root cause is a spec ambiguity—“subscription start date” could mean the signup date (Jan 15) or the billing period start (Feb 1). Engineering implemented Feb 1, and the fix is a small 1-line UI change (label or show both dates).

scenarios.name: lunch_decision
scenarios.response_text:
B

Reason: The Billing Migration target is March 31, with ongoing reconciliation and webhook-format lock requirements. Introducing annual billing would require proration/refund/dunning changes and associated webhook adjustments, which would conflict with the March 31 cutover and the current blockers (reconciliation and webhook format).

scenarios.name: postmortem_reframe
scenarios.response_text: B — Monitoring detected the issue in 12 minutes (09:12 alert), and the root cause is a process gap: the migration wasn’t tested against production-scale data with historical records, notably legacy accounts missing plan_change_date.

scenarios.name: rate_limit_ghost
scenarios.response_text:
C

Explanation: Baseline combined usage is 60 + 35 = 95 req/sec, which is 95% of the 100 req/sec limit. During spikes (1.5–2x), 143–190 req/sec exceeds the limit, causing 429 errors.
